title: Chief of Staff Product Operating System Specification
summary: Implemented the central Decision Card contract from the v3 chief-of-staff product spec. Todos now project through a shared action-card layer with person/company/relationship context, why-now reasoning, source evidence, source-health disclosure, prepared action hints, stale keep-or-dismiss handling, safe buttons, and product scoring. Telegram todo cards and the dashboard review card now use that contract, and the operational Telegram verification loop includes an action-card 10/10 gate.
next_action: Monitor production behavior and use user feedback to broaden Decision Cards into meeting prep, mobile field cards, and richer prepared draft approval flows.

verification.notes: Compiled with warnings-as-errors. Focused tests passed for ActionCards, Telegram todo actions, and dashboard review cards. Telegram chief-of-staff verification loop passed score=10/10 with the new action_card_product_contract scenario.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Built and verified the shared Decision Card implementation across todo projection, Telegram todo cards, dashboard review cards, and the operational verification loop.
pending_steps: Watch production todo cards for overlong copy or missing relationship context.
Extend the same card contract to meeting prep and mobile API surfaces.
Add approval-first draft actions directly from todo cards.
open_questions: Should web search be automatic for unfamiliar business contacts, or only user-approved?
Should family/personal priority be configurable in v1, or part of the default product stance?
How much should Maraithon explain ranking decisions to the user?
Should review sessions optimize for all open loops, top N, or time available?
Should the product expose a what-Maraithon-learned surface after every review or only when meaningful?
How assertive should Maraithon be when it thinks the user is ignoring something for good reason?
Where is the line between useful prepared action and overstepping?
last_reviewed_sections: Microsoft Work Trend Index infinite workday
resume_context.notes: Existing unrelated working tree changes were left untouched.

history.note: Created draft product gap review spec for on-the-go chief-of-staff workflows.

Rewrote the draft into a concrete chief-of-staff action operating system spec with critical findings, product decisions, contracts, state model, ranking policy, workflows, rollout phases, and validation matrix.
Rewrote the spec as a product-first chief-of-staff doctrine grounded in online research and focused on why/what rather than implementation mechanics.

Implemented the shared Decision Card projection, wired it into Telegram and dashboard todo review surfaces, added a verification-loop contract, and passed focused tests plus score=10/10 operational verification.
